challenges.title: Div and Span Basics
challenges.description: Create a page that uses <div> for block-level grouping and <span> for inline styling. Include at least two <div> sections each containing text, and use <span> inside a paragraph to highlight a word with a different color via a <style> block. Assign your code to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Use div for block grouping and span for inline styling -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert html.lower().count('= 2, 'Should contain at least two
elements' assert html.lower().count('
') >= 2, 'Should contain at least two closing tags' assert ' element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a closing tag' assert '
hints: <div> is a block-level container that takes up the full width.
<span> is an inline container used to style a portion of text within a line.

challenges.title: Styled Layout with Divs and Spans
challenges.description: Create a page layout with at least three styled <div> sections (using class selectors with background-color, padding, and margin). Inside one section, use <span> elements with different classes to demonstrate inline styling (e.g., bold text, colored text). Assign your code to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a layout with styled divs and spans -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<style>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <style> block'
assert html.lower().count('<div') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three <div> elements'
assert html.lower().count('<span') >= 2, 'Should contain at least two <span> elements'
assert 'background-color' in html.lower(), 'Should use background-color on divs'
assert 'padding' in html.lower(), 'Should use padding on divs'
assert 'margin' in html.lower(), 'Should use margin on divs'
assert html.lower().count('class=') >= 3, 'Should use class attributes on at least three elements'
style_block = html.lower().split('<style>')[1].split('</style>')[0]
assert style_block.count('.') >= 3, 'Should define at least three class selectors in CSS'
hints: Give each <div> a class and style it with background-color, padding, and margin.
Use different classes on <span> elements for distinct inline styles like color or font-weight.
